pr_scores.reasoning: This is a moderate refactor moving health check calculations from an older system to Temporal, requiring changes across multiple health check implementations. While the pattern is established, it touches core monitoring infrastructure and requires careful validation that all health checks execute correctly in the new system. The backend migration adds moderate complexity and shared-code risk.

pr_scores.author: arthurdedeus
pr_scores.title: feat(journey-builder): Add templates page for journey builder
pr_scores.one_line_summary: Add a journey templates selection page enabling users to bootstrap customer journey creation with pre-built patterns or existing funnels.
pr_scores.reasoning: This is a new feature adding substantial UI and navigation logic to the journey builder workflow. The implementation introduces new scenes, components, and data flow logic across the customer analytics product. While the individual components are well-structured, the integration into the navigation pipeline and template application logic adds moderate complexity. Good test coverage through snapshots and community review validation.

pr_scores.author: sakce
pr_scores.title: fix(endpoints): massive queries prevent saving columns to DB
pr_scores.one_line_summary: Fix endpoints API hanging by gracefully handling massive queries that fail column extraction, preventing frontend endpoint list from blocking.
pr_scores.reasoning: This is a critical bugfix addressing a production issue where certain queries block the endpoints endpoint entirely. The fix is simple (save empty rows and capture exceptions) but targets an important availability problem. The moderate risk comes from changing error handling behavior in a core API path—needs careful verification that partial/empty column data doesn't cascade into downstream issues.

pr_scores.author: rafaeelaudibert
pr_scores.title: feat: Migrate MCP UI apps from self-contained HTML to Workers Static Assets
pr_scores.one_line_summary: Refactor MCP UI apps build system from self-contained inlined HTML to Cloudflare Workers Static Assets, improving startup performance and maintainability.
pr_scores.reasoning: This is a significant build system and architecture refactor affecting how MCP UI apps are packaged and served. The change addresses real performance concerns (12.5MB HTML strings approaching CPU limits) but involves restructuring the build pipeline and app layout. Moderate complexity in coordinating multiple file movements and build script changes; moderate risk due to deployment infrastructure changes, but well-isolated to the MCP service.

pr_scores.author: rnegron
pr_scores.title: chore(mcp): add lint check for tool name length
pr_scores.one_line_summary: Add CI lint check to enforce MCP tool name length compliance with external client limits (60-char max including server name).
pr_scores.reasoning: This is a lightweight tooling addition that adds a build-time validation step to prevent silent filtering of tools by MCP clients with strict naming constraints. The implementation is straightforward—a new lint script and CI integration with minimal risk and no runtime impact. Addresses operational friction by catching naming issues early in development.

pr_scores.author: clr182
pr_scores.title: fix: conversation tab use unfiltered properties
pr_scores.one_line_summary: Fixes AI conversation tab showing null input/output when PostHog properties are hidden by using unfiltered event data.
pr_scores.reasoning: This is a straightforward one-line logic fix in a single component file. The change is isolated to the EventPropertyTabs display logic and affects only users who enable the hide-PostHog-properties filter while viewing conversation tabs. The risk is minimal since it only changes which data source is used for display without altering any core event processing or data integrity.

pr_scores.author: rnegron
pr_scores.title: fix(mcp): update stale error-tracking snapshot blocking master
pr_scores.one_line_summary: Regenerates stale MCP tool-schema snapshot to unblock CI after schema annotation changes.
pr_scores.reasoning: This is a pure snapshot regeneration with 15 lines added and 1 removed—likely just JSON structure updates. It's purely maintenance to realign snapshots with schema changes from another PR (#51469) and carries zero user impact or technical risk. The work is necessary but trivial.

pr_scores.author: MattBro
pr_scores.title: feat: add org/project picker for agentic authorize flow
pr_scores.one_line_summary: Adds organization and project picker UI for agentic authorization flow, allowing users with multiple orgs to select which one to link via Stripe provisioning.
pr_scores.reasoning: This is a moderate-complexity feature spanning backend and frontend: new SPA scene, new API endpoint for authorization confirmation, and conditional redirect logic based on org count. The user-facing impact is significant (solves a real multi-org onboarding problem), and the changes touch both auth flow and UI. Risk is moderate due to the auth flow modifications—any bugs here could break provisioning—but the scope is contained to the agentic authorization flow. The 16 file changes include many snapshot updates which inflate the diff without adding complexity.

pr_scores.author: rafaeelaudibert
pr_scores.title: feat: Add cancelled state handling to MCP UI AppWrapper
pr_scores.one_line_summary: Adds UI feedback for cancelled tool calls in MCP applications by tracking cancellation state and displaying a distinct message.
pr_scores.reasoning: This is a focused enhancement to the MCP UI AppWrapper that adds state tracking and conditional messaging for a specific tool outcome (cancellation). The changes are localized to two files (hook + component) with only 21 net lines added. It improves user experience by distinguishing cancelled from errored tools, but only affects users of MCP-instrumented applications. The risk is low since it's purely UI-driven with no data or logic mutations.

pr_scores.author: carlos-marchal-ph
pr_scores.title: feat(llma): add Traceloop/OpenLLMetry OTel compatibility
pr_scores.one_line_summary: Adds Traceloop/OpenLLMetry OTel compatibility middleware to normalize pre-spec LLM span attributes into PostHog's gen_ai conventions, enabling proper classification of instrumented LLM applications.
pr_scores.reasoning: This is a solid feature addition with 525 net lines of middleware logic, tests, and attribute mapping across TypeScript and Rust. It solves a real integration gap (Traceloop covers 20+ LLM libraries but sends non-standard OTel spans). The complexity is moderate—pattern matching and attribute reassembly—and follows existing middleware conventions well. Risk is moderate since it's ingestion-path code but the changes are well-tested and don't affect existing event flows. The novelty is high as it enables a new integration class.

pr_scores.author: carlos-marchal-ph
pr_scores.title: feat(llma): add observability to OTel ingestion path
pr_scores.one_line_summary: Adds comprehensive observability instrumentation (histograms, counters, error breakdowns) to the OTel ingestion path to enable monitoring and debugging of AI span processing.
pr_scores.reasoning: This is a clean observability enhancement adding tracing instrumentation, metrics counters, and histograms to the Rust capture handler. The complexity is moderate—typical observability plumbing with histogram bucketing and label dimensions—and the changes are isolated to monitoring/metric collection without altering event processing logic. The risk is minimal since these are additive metrics that don't affect the happy path. The value is primarily operational (better production visibility) rather than user-facing.

pr_scores.author: darkopia
pr_scores.title: feat: first last seen info icons
pr_scores.one_line_summary: Adds clarifying tooltips to 'First seen' and 'Last seen' labels on the event definition page to explain they represent ingestion timestamps, not event timestamps.
pr_scores.reasoning: This is a minimal UI enhancement adding informational tooltips to reduce user confusion. The change is isolated to a single component (+12/-2), touches no logic or shared code, and has negligible risk. While improving UX clarity, it's essentially documentation-in-UI rather than a functional feature or behavioral change.

pr_scores.author: sampennington
pr_scores.title: chore(notebooks): Adds e2e notebooks tests
pr_scores.one_line_summary: Adds end-to-end Playwright tests for notebooks feature to catch basic regressions in insight addition and configuration workflows.
pr_scores.reasoning: This is test coverage for an existing feature area (+317 lines of test code across three files). While adding valuable regression protection for a feature that has been buggy, it's routine test work with zero production risk. The scope is limited to the notebooks feature, and the complexity is straightforward test orchestration rather than deep algorithmic or architectural challenges.

pr_scores.author: gustavohstrassburger
pr_scores.title: feat: enforce ENABLE_REALTIME_COHORT_EVALUATION env var for realtime cohort processing
pr_scores.one_line_summary: Enforces the `ENABLE_REALTIME_COHORT_EVALUATION` environment variable in feature flag evaluation to prevent incorrect caching of realtime cohorts and enable dynamic evaluation.
pr_scores.reasoning: This is a critical correctness fix in the core feature flag evaluation pipeline (Rust backend). It requires threading configuration state through multiple layers (Config → Context → Matcher) and changes evaluation behavior conditionally, introducing moderate architectural complexity. The risk is real but well-mitigated: the change only affects behavior when a specific env var is explicitly configured, includes comprehensive test updates, and has been reviewed by the platform team. It directly impacts feature flag accuracy for customers using realtime cohorts.

pr_scores.author: nickbest-ph
pr_scores.title: fix(personhog): metrics need buckets
pr_scores.one_line_summary: Fixes histogram metric bucket configuration in personhog-router and personhog-replica to use millisecond-appropriate buckets, making latency dashboards usable again.
pr_scores.reasoning: This is a targeted performance/observability fix with trivial implementation (+10 lines configuring bucket boundaries). While the impact is broad (affects all latency monitoring across two services), the change is safe and isolated—simply correcting bucket configuration that was always wrong. The fix directly addresses a customer-facing observability problem where dashboards were returning `+Inf` values due to millisecond observations being measured against second-scaled buckets.

pr_scores.author: christiaan-ph
pr_scores.title: fix(ui): ignore Enter key during IME composition
pr_scores.one_line_summary: Adds IME composition detection to Enter key handlers in LemonInput and LemonTextArea to prevent submitting forms while users are selecting characters in CJK input methods.
pr_scores.reasoning: This is a minimal, surgical bugfix (+2/-2) addressing a real usability problem for CJK language users. The solution (`isComposing` check) is a standard browser API pattern with no risk to existing behavior—it simply prevents premature form submission during composition. While the scope is limited to two input components, the impact is meaningful for accessibility and international users.

pr_scores.author: hpouillot
pr_scores.title: feat(hogvm): implement JSONExtract in TypeScript and Python STLs
pr_scores.one_line_summary: Implements the `JSONExtract` function across TypeScript, Python HogVM, and JavaScript compiler STLs to achieve parity with the existing Rust implementation and unblock error tracking product.
pr_scores.reasoning: This is a cross-platform feature implementation ensuring HogQL consistency across runtime environments. The complexity is moderate—implementing the same function three times across different languages/contexts with careful test case alignment. Risk is low because it fills a documented gap rather than changing existing behavior, but there's some care required in porting Rust logic correctly to Python and TypeScript. The novelty is modest (implementing existing logic in new places) but strategically important since error tracking depends on this function.

pr_scores.author: arthurdedeus
pr_scores.title: feat(conversations): Add support tickets notebook node for person profiles
pr_scores.one_line_summary: Adds a notebook node for support tickets in person profiles, enabling comprehensive customer interaction views by refactoring existing ticket scene code for reuse.
pr_scores.reasoning: This is a feature that extends the notebooks capability to include support tickets, moderately complex due to refactoring shared components and integrating with multiple subsystems (persons, notebooks, conversations). The scope affects customer analytics workflows but is isolated to a specific feature area. Moderate risk exists around ensuring the refactored SuportTicketsScene doesn't break existing ticket viewing, though the approval suggests testing was thorough.

pr_scores.author: yasen-posthog
pr_scores.title: feat(notifications): add NotificationEvent model, migration, and product scaffold
pr_scores.one_line_summary: Bootstraps a new notifications product with foundational data models (NotificationEvent, NotificationReadState) and enums, establishing the infrastructure for targeted in-app notifications across users, teams, organizations, and roles.
pr_scores.reasoning: This is a major new capability establishing the data layer for the notifications system. High complexity due to careful design of the targeting model (target_type enums, resolved_user_ids snapshots), access control integration (resource_type filtering), and migration management. The scope is broad as notifications will eventually affect all users. High risk requires careful attention to access control (IDOR checks noted in files changed) and migration correctness, though the scaffolding approach minimizes immediate user-facing impact.

pr_scores.author: webjunkie
pr_scores.title: chore(ci): consolidate trivial workflow jobs to reduce runner spin-ups
pr_scores.one_line_summary: Consolidates 12 trivial single-purpose GitHub Actions jobs into existing jobs across 10 workflows to reduce runner spin-up overhead by ~10-20s per removed job.
pr_scores.reasoning: This is routine CI/CD optimization with negligible technical complexity—pure YAML reorganization with no functional changes to builds or tests. The change is entirely safe and isolated to workflow definitions, with clear trade-offs documented. Net negative line count and straightforward review comments indicate low risk, though it requires domain familiarity with GitHub Actions.

pr_scores.author: jurajmajerik
pr_scores.title: feat(experiments): Add is_precomputed field to experiment query response
pr_scores.one_line_summary: Adds an `is_precomputed` boolean field to experiment query responses to signal whether exposures were served from the precomputed computation path.
pr_scores.reasoning: Low complexity—a simple boolean flag addition to the query response schema affecting only the experiments feature area. Minimal code changes across schema definitions (frontend, backend, MCP) with no migration or business logic complexity. Safe and isolated change that provides useful diagnostic information to the frontend without risk to existing functionality.

pr_scores.author: jurajmajerik
pr_scores.title: feat(experiments): polish timeseries chart and enable timeseries for demo data
pr_scores.one_line_summary: Polishes the experiments timeseries chart visualization (angled labels, padding, color adjustments) and enables timeseries for demo experiments.
pr_scores.reasoning: Low complexity—pure frontend UI tweaks (CSS/styling) and demo data configuration with no algorithmic or architectural changes. Isolated to the experiments visualization component with trivial risk. The changes are cosmetic improvements with high user-facing value, justified by visual testing rather than unit tests, appropriate for this type of work.

pr_scores.author: havenbarnes
pr_scores.title: fix(email): text editing tools hiding content
pr_scores.one_line_summary: Fixes a UI bug where the email templater's text editing panel hides the first line of content by repositioning the panel outside edited blocks.
pr_scores.reasoning: Single-file bugfix addressing a cosmetic but user-facing issue in the email editor. Minimal risk with no shared code impact or data integrity concerns. The fix is localized to panel positioning logic and verified through manual testing across different block sizes, making it a straightforward quality improvement.

pr_scores.author: havenbarnes
pr_scores.title: fix(workflows): broken e2e test

The fix is isolated to the test file and one component, with no production logic changes. It's routine maintenance of test infrastructure.

pr_scores.author: andrewm4894
pr_scores.title: feat(llma): add sentiment tab for scanning user messages
pr_scores.one_line_summary: Add new Sentiment tab to LLM Analytics for scanning user messages by sentiment with card-based UI and context expansion.
pr_scores.reasoning: This is a substantive feature addition (~955 lines) introducing a new UI tab, sentiment querying logic, and utility functions within LLM Analytics. The work involves new SQL queries, frontend components, and feature flagging, representing genuine product capability enhancement. Risk is moderate due to backend query changes and new customer-facing UI patterns, but changes are contained within the LLM Analytics feature area.

pr_scores.author: webjunkie
pr_scores.title: chore(devex): reduce OpenAPI schema generation warnings
pr_scores.one_line_summary: Reduce OpenAPI schema generation warnings by adding type hints to serializer fields and introducing PostHogAutoSchema pattern.
pr_scores.reasoning: While the line count is substantial (~1000 lines), this is primarily systematic annotation work across 55 files adding `@extend_schema_field` decorators and a reusable schema pattern. The changes improve developer experience and CI signal clarity rather than altering runtime behavior. Risk is minimal since these are declarative additions and infrastructure improvements to schema generation.

pr_scores.author: fercgomes
pr_scores.title: feat(onboarding): add flag to hide navbar
pr_scores.one_line_summary: Add feature flag to conditionally hide navbar during onboarding for user behavior experimentation.
pr_scores.reasoning: This is a lightweight feature (26 lines) introducing a simple conditional flag (`onboarding-navbar`) to gate navbar visibility. The change is low-risk and isolated to navigation logic, enabling A/B testing of the signup-to-settings flow. It's a pragmatic experiment harness rather than complex new functionality.

pr_scores.author: orian
pr_scores.title: feat: Enable ClickHouse analyzer setting for selected teams
pr_scores.one_line_summary: Enable ClickHouse analyzer setting for selected teams via instance configuration to improve query performance.
pr_scores.reasoning: This feature adds per-team control over ClickHouse query optimization through instance settings, affecting query execution at the data pipeline level. The change spans backend query logic, settings, and includes test coverage (~145 lines). Risk is elevated due to query performance implications and potential impact on teams if analyzer behavior changes, though the feature flag mechanism provides safety.

pr_scores.author: vdekrijger
pr_scores.title: fix: increase page load timeout for exports
pr_scores.one_line_summary: Increase page load timeout for exports from headless Chrome to reduce screenshot-capture failures.
pr_scores.reasoning: This is a single-line parameter adjustment (timeout increase) to the export image exporter, directly addressing observed timeouts in production. The fix is trivial, low-risk, and surgical—increasing a timeout threshold is a safe operational adjustment. It's routine maintenance responding to production telemetry.

pr_scores.author: veryayskiy
pr_scores.title: fix: race condition for tickets
pr_scores.one_line_summary: Eliminates race condition in ticket restoration by preventing concurrent loadTickets/restoreFromUrlToken calls and skipping cache for empty results.
pr_scores.reasoning: This addresses a real concurrency bug with moderate complexity—coordinating async operations and fixing server-side caching logic. The impact is scoped to the conversations product but critical for users relying on URL-based ticket restoration. The fix requires careful judgment around operation ordering and cache invalidation, with meaningful risk if the finally-block logic doesn't execute as expected.

pr_scores.author: veryayskiy
pr_scores.title: fix: dashboard overlap
pr_scores.one_line_summary: Fixes UI overlap issue in dashboard filter panel with minimal CSS/layout adjustments.
pr_scores.reasoning: This is a trivial UI fix (7 lines added/removed) isolated to a single component with no logic changes. The impact is purely cosmetic—improving visual presentation of filters. There is virtually no risk; the change is safe and well-reviewed.

pr_scores.author: rodrigoi
pr_scores.title: feat(experiments): summarize experiment session replays skill.
pr_scores.one_line_summary: Introduces a new AI skill for LLM-powered experiment session replay summarization, replacing a MaxTool-based approach.
pr_scores.reasoning: This is a moderately complex feature integrating new LLM capabilities with the experiments module. The scope is focused (experiments + AI), but the novelty is high—it's architecting a new skill for the AI system. Risk is low because it's behind a feature flag for production testing, and reviewers acknowledged the limitations (skill won't be used immediately). Most changes are snapshot updates and configuration.

pr_scores.author: VojtechBartos
pr_scores.title: feat(mcp): add alerts MCP tools
pr_scores.one_line_summary: Adds comprehensive MCP tools for alert management (CRUD operations) and enriches alert serializers with descriptions for AI agent integration.
pr_scores.reasoning: This is a substantial feature addition (1967 lines) enabling AI agents to manage alerts via MCP protocol—a new capability for the alerts system. Complexity is moderate-to-high due to coordinating backend tools, schema generation, and frontend integration. Impact spans multiple services (alerts, MCP, HogQL). Risk is moderate since it involves schema changes and new API surface area, though the change is well-structured with generated code and proper field documentation.

pr_scores.author: estefaniarabadan
pr_scores.title: fix(ws): clear s3fs instance cache
pr_scores.one_line_summary: Fixes intermittent 'Event loop is closed' RuntimeError in V3 data imports by clearing s3fs instance cache before async operations.
pr_scores.reasoning: This is a targeted critical bugfix addressing a real production crash in the data import pipeline. The fix is simple (8 lines) but required understanding of s3fs lifecycle and async event loop management. Scope is limited to the V3 import processor but critical for reliability. The risk is low—adding a cache clear is safe and isolated—but the judgment required to diagnose the async/caching interaction justifies the moderate risk score.

pr_scores.author: MattPua
pr_scores.title: chore(dashboard): pass zoom level for dashboard resize and move events
pr_scores.one_line_summary: Enhances dashboard event tracking by including zoom level in resize and move event telemetry.
pr_scores.reasoning: This is a straightforward telemetry enhancement (10 lines changed) with minimal complexity—passing an additional parameter through event logging. Impact is internal/observational; it doesn't change user-facing behavior. The change is safe and isolated, requiring no special engineering judgment. It's maintenance-level work that improves data quality for analytics.

pr_scores.author: pauldambra
pr_scores.title: feat(replay): add tier-aware rate limiting to session recording listing API
pr_scores.one_line_summary: Implements tier-aware rate limiting for the session recording list API with improved observability metrics.
pr_scores.reasoning: This adds differentiated rate limiting logic based on customer tier to a core API endpoint, requiring careful consideration of backward compatibility and monitoring. The change affects a frequently-used listing endpoint used across different client types (web app, sharing, personal API keys), making the risk moderate due to shared-code implications and the need for proper metrics to detect issues. While not groundbreaking, it's a valuable feature that improves platform scalability and fairness across tiers.

pr_scores.author: oliverb123
pr_scores.title: feat(sig): add a skill and docs around querying signals via the mcp
pr_scores.one_line_summary: Adds documentation and skills to help PostHog AI query Signals data via the MCP interface.
pr_scores.reasoning: This is pure documentation and skill definitions with no code logic changes or backend impact. The 339 added lines are entirely documentation/reference material. While it enhances the AI capability for querying signals, it carries minimal risk and is straightforward knowledge-base expansion for the MCP integration.

pr_scores.author: sampennington
pr_scores.title: fix(insights): Compare to previous showing wrong date
pr_scores.one_line_summary: Fixes incorrect date labels on x-axis when using 'compare to previous period' in insights by ensuring consistent series sorting.
pr_scores.reasoning: This addresses a data correctness bug affecting chart visualization where users see wrong period dates—a critical UX issue for data analysis. The fix requires moving logic to Kea state management for consistent series ordering, which touches multiple chart components and has moderate shared-code risk. The fix is well-tested and reviewer-approved, making it a justified critical bugfix despite moderate complexity.

pr_scores.author: anirudhpillai
pr_scores.title: fix(retention): disable cumulative mode for 24h rolling windows

The fix is surgical—affecting only the retention insight UI and query validation—with clear test coverage and no risk of breaking valid use cases. It's a critical UX fix despite being simple in scope, as it prevents users from generating incorrect insights.
